prompt.content: SteelTreeSolutions (STS) has filed a claim against Sweetmarch Build Partners (SBP) for breach of contract. STS is alleging that SBP has failed to pay $500,000 for work it performed as a subcontractor. In its complaint, STS alleges all of the essential elements required for a breach of contract complaint.  It does not allege anything else. SBP files a demurrer. Should it be granted? Assume that jurisdiction and venue have been properly alleged. Provide an explanation.

rubrics.name: States that SteelTreeSolutions did not allege that it was a licensed contractor (ver_4f390d13c4714138a636b180c050cb2b)

messages.content: States that SteelTreeSolutions did not allege that it was a licensed contractor.

rubrics.name: States that Sweetmarch Build Partners' demurrer should be granted (ver_82aa89ee6a57415285673c61e55985ee)

messages.content: States that Sweetmarch Build Partners' demurrer should be granted.

golden_responses.golden_text_response: Sweetmarch Build Partners' demurrer should be granted because SteelTreeSolutions did not allege that it was a licensed contractor.
